prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "90/90 髋开合" (90/90 hip switch position) exercise, front-three-quarter view from above and slightly to the side. Person posture: Athletic person seated on the floor in a 90/90 hip position: the right leg is in front with the right hip externally rotated, right knee bent at 90 degrees with the right thigh perpendicular to the body (knee pointing out to the right side) and the right shin lying horizontally across the body. The left leg is behind to the left side with the left hip internally rotated, left knee bent at 90 degrees with the left thigh angled to the left and the left shin lying horizontally pointing backward (knee pointing forward, foot pointing back). Torso upright with hands either on the floor at the sides or on the front knee. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: hip rotators (deep external and internal rotators) of both hips, with stretch on the gluteal complex. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: adductors and hip flexors and gluteus medius. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "髋外旋肌群" pointing to the external hip rotators; "臀大肌 / 臀中肌" pointing to the gluteal muscles; "内收肌群 / 髂腰肌" pointing to the adductor and hip flexor area. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
